title: eriogonum microtheca subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum microtheca subsp. typicum, commonly known as the desert buckwheat, is a plant species within the Eriogonum genus, characterized by its shrub-like appearance and small white to pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum microtheca subsp. typicum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to promote healing and soothe irritated skin.
